label: 210_middle east_east_middle_sectarian
description: The United States deployed 1700 Marines to Beirut in 1958 amid sectarian tensions, reflecting its historical involvement in the Middle East. The intervention aimed to prevent a potential communist takeover and maintain stability in Lebanon.

central_articles.title: What to do – and what not to do – in the Middle East
central_articles.excerpt: For over a decade, the United States has sought to wind down the wars in Afghanistan and Iraq, reduce its military footprint in the Middle East, and redirect scarce resources to Asia. Global and regional trends reinforced this American desire to reduce the priority of the Middle East in its global strategy, and the…

central_articles.title: Beirut 1958: America’s origin story in the Middle East
central_articles.excerpt: At 3pm on July 15, 1958, 1,700 U.S. Marines stormed the beaches of Beirut. They were ready for combat, weapons loaded, and backed up by a full 70 warships in the Mediterranean Sea (including three aircraft carriers: the USS Essex, USS Wasp, and USS Saratoga). Back in the United States, the 82nd Airborne Division was…
